claimText: Smog-eating technology uses photocatalytic activity driven by light energy to break down harmful pollutants and organic waste.
evidenceExcerpt: The technology uses light energy (photocatalytic activity) to break down harmful pollutants and organic waste.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of smog-eating technology.
distractorHooks: Uses thermal energy instead of light energy
Absorbs pollutants without breaking them down

claimText: Smog-eating coatings typically utilize titanium dioxide (TiO2) as a photocatalyst under UV light to decompose nitrogen oxides (NOx).
evidenceExcerpt: It typically utilizes titanium dioxide (TiO2) coatings under UV light to decompose nitrogen oxides (NOx).
examUse: Statement-based question on the chemical components of smog-eating coatings.
distractorHooks: Uses sulfur dioxide instead of titanium dioxide
Operates under infrared light instead of UV light

claimText: The primary objective of applying smog-eating photocatalytic coatings on roads is to reduce nitrogen dioxide (NO2) and harmful hydrocarbons in public spaces.
evidenceExcerpt: Delhi Government is studying Smog Eating photocatalytic coatings on roads to reduce NO2 and hydrocarbons.
examUse: Statement-based question on the target pollutants of smog-eating roads.
distractorHooks: Targets greenhouse gases like CO2 or methane instead of NO2 and hydrocarbons
